ab: miwari u harem kana wari?
ch: M:你今天有空嘛?
en: Are you available today?

ab: kemuda diyamaw?
ch: W:怎麼了?
en: What is the matter?

ab: maranger ku mukuwa mudadangiya kannu.
ch: M:我想去拜訪你。
en: I would like to visit you.

ab: ulra ku vangavangan harem kana wari, nu ’emanan lra miwari ku lra!
ch: W:我今天有事要忙，明天我才有空了！
en: I am out today. I won’t be free until tomorrow.

ab: nu kemadu mu, kayku lra mena’uwa kannu nu ’emanan piya ta lra kare’alri maw!
ch: M:那麼,我明天下午再去探望你了喔！
en: Then, are you available tomorrow? I’ll visit you tomorrow afternoon.

ab: ai, ku ngarayaw u lra i ruma’.
ch: W:可以，我在家等你。
en: Sure, I will wait for you at home.

ab: kiyumalra ku diya, karuwa ku pasirep kananu ngai? kuy valrayaw kema ku.
ch: M:請問我可以錄音嗎?我想做紀錄。
en: Would you mind if  I do the voice recording? I would like to keep a record.

ab: ai! kuwaceng da eman!
ch: W:好啊!有何不好!
en: Sure! Why not.

ab: karuwa ku pasirep kani di kananu dadek?
ch: M:我可以錄這裡及你的身影嗎?
en: Would you mind if I do the video recording of here and you?

ab: yi ! adi ku piyamau di, adi inapecan ini na soko, manan.
ch: W:哦!我又不好看，倉庫也沒整理，不要啦。
en: No, I am plain and the warehouse hasn’t been tidied up yet.

ab: nu kemadu mu, ku siyasingaw maruwa lra.
ch: M:那我拍照就好。
en: Then I will just take photos.

ab: kuwaceng! amana siyasingaw a!
ch: W:不好！別拍照啦！
en: No! Don’t take photos!

ab: ai! lrayuwan kanu muwai u diya kipakiumalr kaningku.
ch: M:好吧！謝謝你願意讓我訪問。
en: Alright! Thank you for accepting the interview.

ab: ikaira! kiyaumalr u da eman diyamaw?
ch: W:不用客氣！你想問什麼?
en: You are welcome. What would you like to know?

ab: kiyaumalr ku diya kannu da lralrak u diya mu, aeman nanu kiyakarunan?
ch: M:請問您年輕時是做什麼的?
en: What do you do when you are young?

ab: kanggohu ku adiyu diya, harem lra mu, toysiw ku lra.
ch: W:我以前是護士，但我現在退休了。
en: I was a nurse but I am retired now.

ab: ulra nu kiyakarunan harem?
ch: M:那你現在有工作嗎?
en: Then, are you working at the moment?

ab: kiyakarun ku i kungkuwan.
ch: W:我在學校上班。
en: I am working at school.

ab: nanu alrak mu, ulra kikadu i dekalr?
ch: M:您的孩子有住在部落嗎?
en: Do your kids live in the tribe too?

ab: adi, kaadu i Tokay ku alrak.
ch: W:不，我的孩子在都市居住。
en: No, my kids are all living in the city.

ab: sasa u na kaadu i ruma’ harem?
ch: M:那你自己一個人住嗎?
en: Then, do you live by yourself?

ab: ulra diya ku suwan a sasaya, di sasaya a ’ayam, mau lra nangku rinuma’enan nadu mu!
ch: W:我還有養一隻狗和一隻鳥，牠們也是我的家人啊！
en: I also keep a dog and a bird. They are my family.

ab: ulra ku vinaaw a ngiyaw saya, mi’avalr ku da haramhaman mu, adi kasalrengseng.
ch: M:我也有養一隻貓，有動物陪伴不會無聊。
en: I have a cat too. I won’t get bored with the company of my pet.

ab: maranger ku kiumalr da kapalisilisi na pararawayan.
ch: M:我想請問你關於打獵的禁忌。
en: I would like to ask you about the taboos of hunting.

ab: adi ku kamadalraman, aymu, karuwa ku tumuvang daku kinadalraman.
ch: W:我不太懂，但我能回答我所了解的。
en: I don’t know much about that, but I will tell you everything I know.

ab: miinada mu da eman na pa’a’alrupan? ulra eman na kapalisilisi?
ch: M:你們打獵的武器有什麼禁忌嗎?
en: Do you have any taboos about hunting weapons?

ab: naniyam pa’a’alrupan mu, melri nu sasihiwan na vavayan
ch: W:我們打獵的武器不是可以讓女生碰到的。
en: Our hunting weapons are untouchable to women.

ab: na vavayan mu, karuwa marelrang mukuwa pa’alrupa?
ch: M:那女人可以一起去打獵嗎?
en: Then, can women go hunting together?

ab: iya! salaw palisi kemadu, adi karuwa pa’alrup na vavayan.
ch: W:不，那是非常犯忌的，女人不可以打獵。
en: No, that’s a violation. Women cannot go hunting.
